compositional_truth_check.findings: P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) is present. P1 (String of Nickels) is present. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) appear to be combined in the lower black pot, though the specimen is struggling significantly.

plant_audit.p1_string_of_nickels: Stable, though showing signs of slow growth and minor leaf desiccation.
plant_audit.p2_p4_mexican_mint_silver_guest: Severe decline. The primary stems are largely necrotic or absent, with only a single viable leaf remaining in the current frame.
plant_audit.p3_pothos: The Pothos leaf is showing progressive chlorosis and necrosis at the margins. The white rabbit anchor remains stable.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Soil appears consistently damp across all pots. No fungal blooms detected, but surface debris (white perlite/mineral deposits) is static.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant organic debris or external contamination.

temporal_deltas.t_minus_5_to_current: Progressive leaf senescence observed in P3. The Pothos leaf has transitioned from a healthy green to a yellowed, necrotic state. The P2/P4 pot has lost structural integrity, with the remaining leaf showing signs of wilting.

visual_health_inference.reasoning: The consistent yellowing and browning of the Pothos leaf (P3) and the near-total loss of foliage in the P2/P4 pot suggest a failure in the root-to-shoot nutrient transport or potential over-saturation of the substrate.

anomalies: Presence of white mineral/perlite debris on soil surface.
Electronic sensor probe in P2/P4 pot shows no signs of recent soil disturbance.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of physiological retreat. The Pothos (P3) is exhibiting classic signs of stress, likely due to environmental factors or root-zone issues, evidenced by the spreading necrosis on the leaf. The P2/P4 container is effectively a 'Systemic Loss' scenario, as the plant material has failed to thrive and is now reduced to a single, fragile leaf. The String of Nickels (P1) remains the most resilient, though it is not actively flourishing.
